With the new update, getting above +12 gives the new rewind fragments, which is great news for many players. As a ""downside"", it no longer gives leveling mats, which was apparently a big incentive for the more very-high investment accounts. An easy way to fix that and let every player have their wanted reward, leveling mats could be added in the rewind fragment cores, matching the old rates
Yeah, as a whale, the Gnosis and Refined Gnosis Fragments were a way better reward.

Thought from Aria in Mythag uni, who have every character +12 :
> This new system is quite bad for new whales. New whales no longer benefit from the leveling mats available here, which was something that allowed them to catch up to old whales over time. With no extra Gnosis fragments here, it is basically impossible for any new whale to actually catch up. This will obviously frustrate newer whales, as they are heavily disincentivized to pull extra with the new system in place, and extremely few whales ever participate in the Alumni Donation fund due to the return being basically non-existent for the amount of money put in.
And it's bad for old whales too, what do we do with all the characters +12? Are we forced to reduce the amount of money spending on this game?
plus, what do we do with this shit, it's meaningless now

Chess is fundamentally a game with completely different rules from morimens. Forcing players to play this kind of chess will be a heavy burden for non-ches players or newcomers. 

Therefore, to solve this problem, I believe we need very low-difficulty bot matches where you can accumulate wins without receiving chess points. If such bot matches are introduced, I think the influx into chess that Biav intended could be achieved more easily and with less resistance. I would like to hear your opinion on this.

This is a side note, but there are also issues with the ranking rewards. Unlike emoticons, which are merely for personal satisfaction, this involves chess being integrated into the core system of Morinens. There is even a problem where what was originally intended for PvE turns into PvP. While this is a lower priority, I believe it requires in-depth consideration. this is not only for chess.

After listening to the opinions of other players in the Korean community, most responded that the chess victory missions are a heavy burden, and that the ranking rewards are also a little tiring because they have to compete with others. Since opinions regarding the rankings were somewhat divided, I would appreciate it if you could focus more on the victory side.

It would be good if you could choose to go into a bot match

We already have bots, but they only appear randomly at low ranks

Fighting bots helps new players build confidence in their skills before matching with other players

See title. I and many others play this game for the PVE Slay the Spire gameplay, because that is what is advertised on the store page. I am not interested in PVP card battling.

I'm sure there are many Traphase players who don't care about Phantasmal Dive either.

Previously, you could focus on one game mode as your favorite endgame content while ignoring the others. Now pulls and PVE progression materials are locked behind PVP.

As a veteran player, this is extremely annoying and makes me want to spend less time playing this game. For new players, this is a hurdle they cannot overcome which will turn into a huge divide between veterans and new players.

A seasonal quest of "win 5 games" also encourages win-trading and non-games, which is equally toxic for the PVP community that actually enjoys the gameplay.

I understand that the current season rewards can no longer be changed. However, for the next season, please consider adjusting seasonal rewards so that players can focus on the content they like and not feel as though they are missing out.

Atleast Pvp players can run pdrive in auto at low level if they hate pdive,but thats not true in reverse .

We (who dont want nothing to do with pvp) HAVE TO WIN 10 TIMES.

atleast ease the process please

I don't want to play this different game of yours BIAV. The rewards are too good for us to miss it but the mode is atrocious, the game already takes long enough to play. 

Let us play our PVE. Thank you.
Please also take all dtide rewards and put them into weekly rewards so I don't have to play the game at all anymore <:24_Clown:1468259444423921871>

Here is the difference.

Dtide only awards progression materials for PvE. Completing high-difficulty Dtide gives you the resources you need to do higher-difficulty PvE stages.

Most of the resources you get from Dtide are also locked behind the easiest difficulty, and the total number of pulls you get is also very small.

In the new Pdive/Traphase/HSR leaderboards, you get 25 pulls per month, which cannot be ignored even if you only play on the lowest difficulty.

You are also forced to play a totally different game you may or may not enjoy, in order to farm progression resources for the game mode you do enjoy. Most of the rewards are locked at the highest leaderboard rank, so by design, 90% of players are going to miss out on it.
There is a difference in wanting players to try different mode and Forcing them.
No one is holding a gun at your head. 🤷‍♂️

You're right, if I feel like the game is screwing me over, I can simply leave and spend my time and money elsewhere.

I am sure it is in BIAV's best interest for this not to happen though.
Not like pvp is the money maker. Annoying paying customers over a passion project isn't the move.
I made a possible suggestion to tackle this problem https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1495675625858535505
These are additional rewards no one of use had before and instead of being happy we get some more dreamsparks/shards you're putting the gun to BIAVs head now lmao.
Except we're not here to play pvp
The rewards from the taboo quest are literally taken from the previous normal weekly quest
or the other gamemode we dont enjoy
why the hell would someone here to play a fun pve card game wanna do pvp. Pdive and Train are whatever because their still pve stiff just take a while to do

We can respect your decision to play PVP. 
So, please respect our right to speak against it.
Thank you.
If you want to add to the conversation in a constructive manner, please feel free to do so.

1. The pulls are not additional rewards as V said, they are the old weekly rewards that have been redistributed.

2. Even if the leaderboard rewards are additional rewards, they encourage a gameplay pattern that is toxic and harmful to the game as a whole.

Now that the rewards are in the game, if you don't get them, you are missing out. Players are incentivized to compete for leaderboard rewards and to play game modes they don't enjoy, because otherwise they will fall behind in account progression, potentially for months or years on end.

If you fall behind in account progression, not only will you have a more difficult time clearing PVE content you want to clear, you also have a more difficult time competing against other players in the HSR leaderboards in all future seasons. This is a huge barrier for new players that will turn people away from the game.

3. This change is also harmful for PVP players.

The fastest way for PVE-only players to do "win X games" seasonal quests is to wintrade. The PVE community outnumbers the PVP community by a large factor. This means that over time, the majority of people on ladder will be people who don't care about Traphase and are going to waste your time instead of playing a real game.

If you care about the health of PVP in this game, you should be concerned about the weekly reward changes as well.

Hello peeps, This is a bit of a feedback post and somewhat of a rant, 
since we now know that there will be a balance patch coming in 2.5.1

I would like to bring about a bit of a discussion about the counter mechanic 
within the game and how I feel like the nobody really knows what to do with it as a whole.

Counter as a mechanic was born when the game was much slower, 
where the meta was exalting as many time as possible, and the damage in general isn't as high as today.
As it is right now, the game is so much faster that the concept of "slowly" ramping up counters doesn't cut it anymore.
Giving rise to the daffodil counter nuke gameplay.

This gameplay isn't exactly traditional counter, It's making as much temporary counters as possible to act as a
"Secondary strength adjacent" to buff attacks. It is basically glorified strength that may or may not benefit from knowing when to burst.

The reason Caecus is such a popular choice and is by far the strongest counter character in the entire game by an unreachable margin
for most character is because of how fast he ramps up and how he scales from tentacle damage, which is super easy to increase in the current
game state.

He is so powerful at generating counter that he invalidates the usage of almost every counter character in game.
To the point where he is memed on as the press 2 cost to turn off brain character.

Buffing any of the counter generation created by any other character will result in caecus becoming way more powerful
Further invalidating the use of any other counter character by default. He is so successful at this his job that no one else sees the light of day.

With his AA increasing his counter generation even further, nobody has the ability to compete with him at all.

Wanda who was supposed to be the de facto counter character has only been getting worse due to the arc 2 mechanic which prevents her from spamming her exalt. Her AA is incredibly out of touch with her play style and base kit.

Her gameplay is meant to be calculating how to properly use her dreamlure and seeing what it is best to use it on.

But now even her AA messes up with her own gameplay, making her cards activate twice actually activates her own leap effects.
meaning if you have less than 5 dreamlure when you activate her 3 or 4 cost card, the second activation will eat 5 stacks post dreamlure generation, messing with her gameplay.

This means the most realistic use of her AA, would be to use it on her own strike or defense to proc her rouse's passive effect.

It is starting to become clear that the concept of permanent counter is getting increasingly harder and harder to balance around.

Buff it too much and the game become almost trivial as you can just try your best to turtle it.
The obvious direction that BIAV doesn't seem to want to go forward with

As such I would suggest just removing the entirely of counters entirely somewhere along the line and rework these characters into something that exists on their own

As it is right now, there is no easy way of fixing or making counters anywhere close to every other mechanic in the game. 

Even thais' exalt generates more counter than most of the counter cast, with the exception of caecus.

This is a general feedback on counter as a mechanic as a whole and the absolute state of the characters tied to this mechanic. I would like to see BIAV find a clear direction for this mechanic in the future or just completely remove it and rework it for something else more creative and better suited for the rhythm that exists within the current state of morimens. 

Maybe with counter gone, nautila, wanda, pandia, caecus, ultra 24 and cost 2 daffodil, will finally be able to be characters that stands on their own legs, instead of being tied together in a doomed tree legged race with zero prizes.
Written sincerely for your consideration <@1131790839291596841>

In https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1494955533382455316/1494955680723898570, it tells us that Fixed Final Verdict benefits from DMG Amplification.

However, as far as I can tell, this is not stated anywhere in game. It is not in the tooltip for Final Verdict, nor anywhere in Arachne's Threads of Fate talent, nor in the description of DMG Amplification.

It would be good to have this crucial information listed in-game so new players will not be confused.

In the reveal post, the information is listed in the Final Verdict tooltip, under the Soulforge Aptitude talent. In game, Arachne's Soulforge Aptitude doesn't have this info (and it doesn't have the Final Verdict tooltip either).

*Winning* 5 matches sounds a lot longer and therefore more frustrating for people who do not actually like Traphase but would like the rewards. 
Changing it to *participation* instead and it can still encourage play (if you must), but at least makes it much quicker and less mentally stressful for people who don't enjoy PvPs?
Can we just change this stupid winning it to have the price to simply participating it, I sucks at pvp and this took me forever <:takenotes:1357186646294138890>

yeah, a lot of people don't like PvP because of loss aversion - in a well balaned PvP game, the win rates should hover around 50% for most players, but the problem is that losing is felt twice as strongly than winning; so basically by playing a 50% win rate game, we're just constantly draining our mental well being...

and locking the rewards behind winning just amplifies this effect... losing a game would feel even worse... so please at least change it so that as long we people take part, it can count towards the rewards...
One button to rule them all.
Also, let us take all the rewards if you click the last one you unlocked, not one at a time.

Pollux's AA has 2 separate effects, both of which currently appear to be bugged:
1. "Sacred Heart" permanently inflicts Bleed damage equal to 100% of its base damage.
2. After "Sacred Heart" is shuffled back into the Draw Pile after being played, its Arithmetica Cost for the current turn becomes 0, and it inflicts an additional 100% Bleed damage.

Effect #1 is currently bugged. This effect only applies on the turn that Pollux Rouses, or on the turn that Sacred Heart is created. After a turn passes, this effect disappears, even though the wording of the AA states that it should be a permanent effect to all "Sacred Heart" cards. Attached is evidence:

- Image #1 shows the 100% bleed effect on Sacred Heart the turn that Pollux plays Rouse
- Image #2 shows the same Sacred Heart after one turn has passed. The bleed effect is now missing

I have highlighted the missing section in red to make it easier to see.

Effect #2 is also bugged. This effect does not stack properly with Effect #1. If Effect #1 is active, then this effect will only occur once, even when "Sacred Heart" is played twice. If Effect #2 is not active, then this effect will stack properly. Attached is evidence:

- Image #1 shows the 3 "Sacred Heart" cards in my hand which will be used for the demonstration of the bug. The first "Sacred Heart" has been retained from a previous turn, while the second and third "Sacred Heart" were created via Pollux's Over-Exalt
- Image #2 shows the effect for the first "Sacred Heart", which is missing the 100% bleed damage from effect #1. I have highlighted this to make it clearer. This "Sacred Heart" has not been played, which is demonstrated by the (1/3) play count on the card
- Image #3 shows the effect once that "Sacred Heart" is played, which can be demonstrated by the (2/3) play count on the card. Because of effect #2, it now inflicts 100% bleed. I have highlighted the number to make it clearer to see
- Image #4 shows the effect once that "Sacred Heart" is played again, which can be demonstrated by the (3/3) play count on the card. Because of effect #2, the second play stacks, increasing the bleed value to 200%. I have highlighted the number to make it clearer to see

Please compare that to the effect of the second "Sacred Heart", which was newly created this turn. 

- Image #1 shows that as the Sacred Heart was just created, effect #1 is working properly and has not been removed yet from the card. I have highlighted the 100% bleed inflict section on the card, which is appplied even though the play count of the card is at (1/3)
- Image #2 shows the effect once that "Sacred Heart" is played, which can be demonstrated by the (2/3) play count on the card. Because of effect #2, it now inflicts an additional 100% bleed. Since effect #1 was already on the card, this means the bleed has now increased to 200%. I have highlighted the number to make it clearer to see. Up until now, this has been working correctly
- Image #3 shows the effect once that "Sacred Heart" is played again, which can be demonstrated by the (3/3) play count on the card. Since it has been played twice, the bleed should have increased by another 100%, like the previous card, which would increase the bleed value to 300%. However, it is still 200%. I have highlighted the number to make it clearer to see

为了方便沟通，我尝试将反馈内容翻译成了中文，希望能更清晰地描述问题。如有表述不当之处，还请见谅。

波吕克斯的最终法则包含两个独立效果，目前看来这两个效果都存在bug：
1. 「圣心」造成 100% 伤害的出血（应为常驻效果）。
2. 「圣心」因打出而洗入抽牌堆后，该「圣心」本回合算力消耗变为 0 且额外造成 100% 伤害的出血。

关于效果#1的bug：
该效果目前仅在波吕克斯开启「灵知觉醒」的当回合，或「圣心」生成的当回合生效。一旦回合结束，该效果就会消失。但根据最终法则的文本描述，这应该是对所有「圣心」牌的常驻强化。
附件证据：
图片#1：显示波吕克斯开启灵知觉醒当回合，「圣心」带有 100% 出血效果。
图片#2：显示同一张「圣心」在过了一个回合后，出血效果消失了。我用红框圈出了缺失的部分。

关于效果#2的bug：
该效果与效果#1无法正确叠加。如果效果#1处于激活状态，那么洗回后的额外出血效果在重复打出时只能触发一次。反之，如果效果#1已经消失（如bug 1所述），该效果反而能正常叠加。
附件证据：
图片#1：手牌中的3张「圣心」。第一张是上一回合留下的，第二、三张是本回合通过「超限」生成的。
图片#2：展示第一张「圣心」（旧牌），此时缺失效果#1的 100% 出血。该牌尚未打出（使用次数 1/3）。
图片#3：打出该牌后，受效果#2影响，它获得了 100% 出血（使用次数 2/3）。
图片#4：再次打出该牌，效果#2成功叠加，出血数值升至 200%（使用次数 3/3）。

对比第二张「圣心」（本回合新生成）：
图片#1：由于是新生成的牌，效果#1正常生效，卡牌带有100%出血（使用次数 1/3）。
图片#2：打出一次后，触发效果#2额外增加100%出血，总计200%。到这一步为止都是正常的（使用次数 2/3）。
图片#3：再次打出该牌，按理说应再次触发效果#2增加100%，使总出血达到300%。然而数值依然停留在200%。我已圈出该数值以便查看。
<@1485258210209435701> 如果方便的话，能不能麻烦你看一下上面的内容并转告给开发人员？目前该效果的表现与描述不符，无论是中文还是英文描述都对不上。非常感谢你的帮助！

While playing with my +12 level 90 Corpo, I noticed that something was strange
I looked into it and indeed, the value of the STR/Tentacle Damage were not the one I predicted
After further analysis I discovered that while Corpo AA Rouse takes effect correctly for the shield value, the displayed STR/Tentacle damage during the turn the Rouse is played is wrong. And doesn't match what actually happens when you discard them.

1st image is me playing her Rouse with the AA effect
2nd image is me passing turn with 2 Pilots made and buffed by the Rouse in hand (I should have won ~24 at turn end !)
3rd image is my team one turn later with only 16 STR won by the Pilots instead of 24
4th image is Casiah with her Dream of the Sea that explains the additional 14 STR won at beginning of battle and 5 after the end of the turn.
I specifically mention the STR but the tentacle damage is also impacted and follow the same logic.
https://tenor.com/view/symboli-rudolf-uma-musume-gesticulate-hand-gestures-anime-gif-10182368678541672035
It would be fine if it was just "participate 5 times" - but 5 wins is pretty time consuming
